proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.1739 on pembrolizumab vs 0.1640 off; OR=1.073, log(OR)=+0.070, chi-square p=0.0034 — small but statistically significant overall benefit.
Response 0.1695 on sotorasib vs 0.1687 off; OR=1.006, p=0.83 — no main effect.
Response 0.1699 on olaparib vs 0.1685 off; OR=1.010, p=0.72 — no main effect.
Response 0.1655 on osimertinib vs 0.1704 off; OR=0.965, p=0.18 — no main effect (numerically lower).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response specifically in egfr_mutation-positive patients (positive interaction term).
treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response specifically in kras_g12c-positive patients (positive interaction term).
treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in brca2_mutation-positive patients (positive interaction term).
treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in tmb_high patients than in tmb_high-negative patients (positive interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: Osimertinib×EGFR-mut interaction coef=+0.024, p=0.76. In EGFR-mut patients, osimertinib RR 0.164 vs 0.166 off (OR=0.99, p=0.87). No biomarker-specific benefit detected.
Sotorasib×KRAS-G12C interaction coef=+0.059, p=0.43. In KRAS-G12C+ patients, sotorasib RR 0.175 vs 0.166 off (OR=1.06, p=0.43). No biomarker-specific benefit.
Olaparib×BRCA2-mut interaction coef=-0.178, p=0.27. In BRCA2-mut patients, olaparib RR 0.146 vs 0.168 off (OR=0.85, p=0.35) — opposite of expected, not significant.
Pembro×TMB-high interaction coef=+0.184, p=4.1e-04. In TMB-high patients, pembro RR 0.197 vs 0.167 off (OR=1.22, p=5e-06); in TMB-low, pembro effect is null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation diminishes the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab (negative interaction term).
egfr_mutation diminishes the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab (negative interaction term).
Within smoking_status strata (current/former/never), the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab differs (smokers benefit more than never-smokers).
Within histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma), the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab differs.
keap1_mutation diminishes the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=-0.211, p=0.0018. In STK11-WT, pembro OR=1.106 (p=1e-04); in STK11-mut, pembro OR=0.896 (p=0.08). STK11 mutation abrogates pembro benefit.
Interaction coef=-0.035, p=0.63. Pembro effect similar in EGFR-mut and EGFR-WT (OR 1.04 vs 1.08). No strong differential.
Pembro × never (vs current) coef=-0.078, p=0.31; Pembro × former coef=-0.009, p=0.86. Numerically smaller pembro effect in never-smokers (OR=1.01) than in smokers (OR=1.08-1.09), but not significant.
Pembro×squamous interaction coef=+0.030, p=0.58. Both histologies show comparable pembro benefit (OR 1.06 adeno; 1.10 squamous). No significant differential.
Interaction coef=-0.058, p=0.36. Pembro benefit slightly attenuated in KEAP1+ (OR 1.02) vs KEAP1- (OR 1.08), not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (positive main effect).
The treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is greater at higher pdl1_tps (positive interaction).
In PD-L1 high (top tertile) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab confers a substantially higher response rate than in PD-L1 low patients.

analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 main effect coef=+0.166 per unit TPS (0–1 scale), p=0.003. Modest overall positive association with response.
Pembro×PD-L1 interaction coef=+0.591, p=1.6e-07. Strong evidence pembro effect grows with PD-L1.
Pembro effect by PD-L1 tertile: low OR=1.02 (p=0.64), med OR=0.94 (p=0.11), high OR=1.29 (p=1e-09). Benefit concentrated in high tertile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response (positive coefficient).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: mean albumin 3.821 in responders vs 3.798 in non-responders (diff=+0.023, t-test p=1.3e-04). Higher albumin associated with response.
mean CRP 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders (diff=-0.37, p=4.2e-04). Higher CRP associated with non-response.
mean weight_loss 3.38% in responders vs 3.93% in non-responders (diff=-0.55, p=2.8e-35). Strong inverse relationship.
logit coef on ecog_ps = -0.375 per unit (p=1.7e-93). Strong negative prognostic effect.
mean LDH 224.3 in responders vs 223.6 in non-responders (diff=+0.6, p=0.50). No association.
mean NLR 3.48 in responders vs 3.50 in non-responders (diff=-0.02, p=0.38). No detectable association.
mean Hb 12.50 in responders vs 12.51 in non-responders (diff=-0.01, p=0.60). No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv patients have lower objective_response than non-stage-IV patients.
has_brain_mets patients have lower objective_response than those without brain metastases.
liver_mets patients have lower objective_response than those without liver metastases.
Common comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, copd, ckd, heart failure) are each associated with lower objective_response.
interstitial_lung_disease_history is associated with lower objective_response (especially with pembro).

analyses.result_summary: stage IV RR 0.154 vs 0.196 (OR=0.74, p=1.1e-33). Strong negative effect.
Brain mets RR 0.144 vs 0.178 (OR=0.78, p=2.0e-18). Strong negative effect.
Liver mets RR 0.173 vs 0.168 (OR=1.03, p=0.34). No significant univariate effect.
Hypertension OR=0.99 p=0.72; diabetes OR=1.01 p=0.76; COPD OR=0.98 p=0.38; CKD OR=1.05 p=0.22; HF OR=1.01 p=0.89; CAD OR=0.94 p=0.05. Comorbidities not strongly associated with response.
ILD-history RR 0.160 vs 0.169 (OR=0.93, p=0.27). No significant univariate effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have higher objective_response than male patients.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
race_ethnicity strata differ in objective_response (omnibus chi-square).
insurance_type strata differ in objective_response (medicaid lower than private).
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with lower objective_response.
rural_residence patients have lower objective_response than urban-residence patients.

analyses.result_summary: Females RR 0.175 vs males 0.164 (OR=1.09, p=5.9e-04). Modest but significant higher response in women.
mean age 65.0 responders vs 65.0 non-responders (diff=+0.05, p=0.67). No detectable age effect.
Response rates: white 0.165, black 0.178, hispanic 0.172, asian 0.177, other 0.185; omnibus chi2 p=0.045. Marginal differential, with white below others.
Response rates: medicaid 0.161, medicare 0.168, private 0.172, uninsured 0.170; omnibus chi2 p=0.23. No significant difference.
mean pack-years 27.3 responders vs 27.4 non-responders (diff=-0.09, p=0.76). No association.
Rural RR 0.167 vs urban 0.170 (OR=0.98, p=0.50). No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 measured SNP variables is associated with objective_response after multiple-testing correction (Bonferroni 0.05/23).
snp_rs7412 (APOE-related) is associated with objective_response (any direction).
snp_rs1050828 (G6PD-related) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Of 23 SNPs, only rs7412 (p=0.018) and rs1050828 (p=0.031) achieved nominal p<0.05; neither survives Bonferroni 0.05/23=0.0022. Genome-wide null.
rs7412 coef=+0.100, p=0.018. Nominally positive, not significant after correction.
rs1050828 coef=-0.099, p=0.031. Nominally negative, not significant after correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy is associated with lower objective_response (selection / refractoriness).
prior_immunotherapy is associated with lower objective_response (immune exhaustion).
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
Longer years_since_diagnosis is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: prior_chemo RR 0.166 vs 0.172 (OR=0.96, p=0.067). Marginal decrease, not statistically significant.
prior_IO RR 0.173 vs 0.168 (OR=1.03, p=0.38). No detectable effect.
Mean lines 1.24 responders vs 1.26 non-responders (diff=-0.016, p=0.24). No detectable effect.
Mean YSD 1.97 responders vs 1.99 non-responders (diff=-0.021, p=0.31). No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit decreases as ecog_ps rises (negative interaction with ecog_ps).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit decreases with greater weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is smaller in stage_iv patients (negative interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is smaller in patients with brain metastases (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Pembro×ECOG interaction coef=-0.036, p=0.32. ECOG strongly prognostic but does not differentially attenuate pembro effect.
Pembro×weight_loss interaction coef=+0.004, p=0.51. No interaction.
Pembro×stage_iv interaction coef=+0.071, p=0.15. Trend toward larger pembro effect in stage IV (opposite of hypothesis), not significant.
Pembro×brain_mets interaction coef=-0.033, p=0.57. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Squamous histology is associated with higher objective_response than adenocarcinoma.
Never-smokers have lower objective_response than current/former smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Squamous vs adeno coef=+0.047, p=0.076. Squamous RR 0.174 vs adeno 0.167 — marginal, not significant.
Former vs current coef=-0.027 p=0.32; Never vs current coef=-0.061 p=0.11. No significant differences.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, stage_iv, brain mets, albumin, weight_loss, sex, the pembro×pdl1_tps interaction term remains positive and significant.
After adjustment, the pembro×stk11_mutation negative interaction remains.
After adjustment, the pembro×tmb_high positive interaction remains.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ pembro*pdl1_tps + pembro*tmb_high + pembro*stk11_mutation + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + sex_female')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: pembro:pdl1_tps coef=+0.593 (p=2e-07); pembro:tmb_high coef=+0.174 (p=0.001); pembro:stk11_mutation coef=-0.203 (p=0.003). All three interactions persist with little change in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one SNP×treatment interaction (across 23 SNPs × 4 treatments = 92 tests) is significant after Bonferroni correction (0.05/92 ≈ 5.4e-4).
snp_rs7412 modifies the response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab (interaction term significant).

analyses.code: for snp,tx: logit('response ~ snp*tx'); collect interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Of 92 SNP×treatment interaction tests, 7 had p<0.05 (vs ~4.6 expected by chance). Smallest p-values rs1800896×sotorasib (p=0.0025) and rs1799853×olaparib (p=0.009). None survive Bonferroni 5.4e-4.
Pembro×rs7412 interaction coef=-0.033, p=0.70. No evidence rs7412 modifies pembro effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib has a positive interaction with msi_high (DNA repair / homologous recombination context).
treatment_olaparib has a positive interaction with tmb_high.
treatment_olaparib has a positive interaction with pten_loss (DNA-repair related).

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib×MSI-high interaction coef=-0.157, p=0.58. No biomarker-specific olaparib benefit.
Olaparib×TMB-high interaction coef=+0.027, p=0.63. No biomarker-specific benefit.
Olaparib×PTEN-loss interaction coef=+0.174, p=0.14. Numerical positive trend, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib reduces the response disadvantage from has_brain_mets (positive interaction with has_brain_mets, since osimertinib has CNS activity).
treatment_osimertinib has a positive interaction with histology=adenocarcinoma vs squamous.

analyses.result_summary: Osimertinib×brain_mets interaction coef=+0.051, p=0.42. No CNS-specific osimertinib benefit detected.
Osimertinib×squamous interaction coef=+0.047, p=0.42. No histology-specific differential.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients derive greater treatment_pembrolizumab response benefit than male patients (positive sex_female × pembro interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Pembro×sex_female interaction coef=+0.146, p=0.0024. In men, pembro OR=1.00 (p=0.94); in women, pembro OR=1.16 (p=2.6e-05). Pembro benefit is concentrated in women.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab response benefit differs across race_ethnicity strata (omnibus interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Individual race × pembro interaction terms not significant (smallest p=0.07 for 'other' vs reference). Pembro ORs: Asian 1.15, Black 1.12, white 1.07, Hispanic 1.08, other 0.82. No statistically significant heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Total number of concurrent treatments (n_tx ∈ {0..4}) is positively associated with objective_response.
Combining treatment_pembrolizumab with treatment_sotorasib produces synergy beyond the sum (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: n_tx coef=+0.015, p=0.23. Response rates: 0 tx 0.162, 1 tx 0.171, 2 tx 0.169, 3 tx 0.171, 4 tx 0.178. Slight numerical trend, not statistically significant.
Pembro×sotorasib interaction coef=+0.058, p=0.25. No detectable synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PD-L1 effect on objective_response differs between squamous and adenocarcinoma histology.

analyses.result_summary: PDL1×squamous interaction coef=+0.011, p=0.93. No histology-dependent PD-L1 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ast_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher heart_rate_bpm is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Bilirubin diff -0.005 (p=0.21). No association.
AST diff -0.24 (p=0.08). Borderline.
CEA diff +0.12 (p=0.29). No association.
Creatinine diff +0.001 (p=0.84). No association.
Heart rate diff -0.05 (p=0.73). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: STK11 mutation specifically dampens the pembro×PD-L1 dose-response relationship (negative three-way interaction pembro:pdl1_tps:stk11_mutation).

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef=-0.388, p=0.22. Numerically large but underpowered. The two-way pembro:pdl1_tps interaction (+0.644) and two-way pembro:stk11 effect remain dominant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for ecog_ps, stage_iv, brain_mets, albumin, crp, weight_loss, sex, age, bmi, histology, smoking_status, race, comorbidities, the prognostic effect of ecog_ps remains the strongest negative predictor (largest |coef|).
After adjustment for clinical and biomarker features, sotorasib, olaparib, and osimertinib show no main response effect.
After adjustment, neither race nor smoking status independently predicts response (i.e., apparent univariate trends disappear).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: ecog_ps=-0.382 (p<1e-19), stage_iv=-0.302 (p<1e-11), has_brain_mets=-0.257 (p<1e-08), albumin=+0.093 (p=1e-04), crp=-0.005 (p=4e-04), weight_loss=-0.039 (p<1e-11). ECOG remains strongest predictor.
Adjusted main effects: sotorasib coef=+0.006 (p=0.80); olaparib coef=+0.011 (p=0.69); osimertinib coef=-0.034 (p=0.20). All null even after adjustment.

Race coefficients (vs Asian ref): black +0.006, hispanic -0.039, other +0.048, white -0.077; none significant. Smoking status (former, never vs current): both p>0.6. Univariate signals attenuated to null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the PD-L1 high (TPS≥0.5) subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response substantially compared with no-pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: chi2 pembro x response in pdl1>=0.5 subgroup
analyses.result_summary: n=10,749 with PDL1≥0.5. Pembro RR 0.209 vs no-pembro 0.156 (OR=1.44, p=7.7e-13). Large absolute difference of ~5.4 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup (pdl1_tps≥0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0), treatment_pembrolizumab dramatically raises objective_response — larger than in any single biomarker subgroup.
In the STK11-mutated subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT raise response (point estimate at or below 1).

analyses.code: chi2 pembro x response in pdl1>=0.5 & tmb_high=1 & stk11=0
analyses.result_summary: n=2,714 in optimal subgroup. Pembro RR 0.313 vs no-pembro 0.153 (OR=2.52, p=1.4e-22). Absolute difference ~16 pp.

analyses.code: chi2 pembro x response in stk11_mutation=1
analyses.result_summary: n=7,451 STK11+. Pembro RR 0.158 vs no-pembro 0.173 (OR=0.90, p=0.084). Numerically lower on pembro; consistent with negative pembro×STK11 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient), consistent with disease burden marker.

After accounting for pembro×sex interaction, the unadjusted female-vs-male main effect on response is fully attributable to women's larger pembro benefit (i.e., conditioning on sex_female=1 yields no main effect).

analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade coef=-0.022, p=0.040. Mild negative association.

After including pembro×sex interaction, sex_female main effect attenuates to coef=+0.008 (p=0.82). The female-vs-male advantage (~+8% univariate OR) is essentially fully explained by larger pembro benefit in women.
